components.title: Shared site header
components.lead: One layout can wrap every page.
paragraphs: This shared block is declared once at the site level instead of being copied into every page.

components.title: Shared site footer
paragraphs: The page-content slot keeps page-specific sections in the middle while shared content stays centralized.

metadata.description: Strict JSON, stable markup, and theme-driven static pages.

components.headline: Announce your app with clean structure
components.subheadline: Strict JSON, fixed components, and theme-driven presentation.

items.title: Model every block explicitly
items.body: Content picks a known component instead of inventing markup.

items.title: Reject extra fields
items.body: Unexpected keys fail before they can drift into production.

items.title: Review small diffs
items.body: Stable HTML keeps generated output easy to inspect.

items.title: Stable markup
items.body: Components own structure, not content JSON.

items.title: Theme discipline
items.body: Themes define exactly one token contract.

components.headline: Build reviewable static pages
components.body: Run validation before every build and keep the markup intentionally boring.

components.headline: Launch with less front-end drift
components.subheadline: A narrow framework is easier to validate, review, and keep consistent.

items.question: Can this framework accept arbitrary HTML from JSON?
items.answer: No. Components own the markup so validation stays strict and predictable.

items.question: Do themes change the structure of a page?
items.answer: No. Themes only change tokens like color, spacing, and typography.
